rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LUCE et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant General Electric Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Luce was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claims against defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LUCE et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden on summary judgment and all issues of summary adjudication. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting her claims against defendant. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s objections on the grounds of defendant¿s failure to submit verifications are sustained. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LUCE et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Ericsson Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Luce was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s failure to demonstrate that plaintiff submitted factually devoid responses to written contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claims against defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to negate an essential element of plaintiff¿s claims against defendant. Even if Mr. Kothe¿s declaration is admissible, when read in conjunction with Mr. Luce¿s deposition testimony it merely creates a triable issue. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LUCE et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Eaton Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff¿s false representation (issue 1) and intentional tort (issue 2) causes of action and claim for punitive damages (issue 3) is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden on all three issues. Plaintiff failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether defendant is liable for false representation, intentional tort or punitive damages. Defendant¿s objections to its General Order 129 interrogatory responses and to Mr. Luce¿s deposition testimony are overruled. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LUCE et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Luce was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claims against defendant, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) As to the government contractor defense argument, defendant did not include this as grounds upon which it based its motion for summary judgment and/or summary adjudication in its notice of motion or separate statement. (CRC 3.1350.) Even if the court does consider defendant¿s argument, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it is entitled to prevail on the government contractor defense. As to punitive damages, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence demonstrating that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. By failing to submit with its moving papers verified factually devoid responses to contention discovery designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support her claim for punitive damages, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff objected on the grounds of no verifications. Where a verification is required, an unverified response is equivalent to no response at all. (Appleton v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 636.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant San Francisco Gravel Co., Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar as to issue 1 (premises owner/contractor liability) and granted as to issue 2 (punitive damages). As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. Plaintiffs represent that they withdraw their premises owner/contractor liability cause of action against this defendant. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, November 1, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Gow Supply Company¿s motion to bifurcate trial on the issue of medical causation is denied.
